arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with winsiderss/systeminformer (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ohpe/juicy-potato (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with libyal/libevt (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: A better strings utility!.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Author: Eric Zimmerman (saericzimmerman@gmail.com) https://github.com/EricZimmerman/bstrings.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bstrings version $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use bstrings because A better strings utility!.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bstrings version $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST` as documented upstream for bstrings.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair bstrings with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bstrings --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
